title: Evangelion 50_ A hidden rabbit not in Annos Hat
display_title: Evangelion 50_ A hidden rabbit not in Annos Hat
wikitext:
[]: Evangelion 5.0_ A hidden rabbit (not) in Anno's Hat.             Quote Post by KLVTZ » Fri Jul 23, 2010 2:24 am Now this is complete speculation, and of course i am by no means implying that this will happen, keep that in mind......... What if Anno decided to release a new sequel after Evangelion 4.0 Final, for example what if he leaves a big cliff hanger, and we don't see "The End" instead we see "To be continued" before credits...... In other words is there a possibility that there could be more sequels than just 4....something that GAINAX isn't saying about. If this would be any possibility,what if he leaves 4.0 right at the ending scene of EoE(or Alternate Scene new and fresh) what would there be to continue? I welcome the community to really speculate a possible ending of 4.0, based on current time line of 1.0 and 2.0, and then say its supposed to continue and speculate on what else is there to continue.. (Your probably thinking this is pointless, but try to see my point)

[]:             Quote Post by esselfortium » Fri Jul 23, 2010 2:46 am I'm not sure I see the point, from a marketing standpoint. Why announce one less than was actually planned? Especially when the movies are already -- for better or worse -- up to around episode 20 of the original timeline.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Fri Jul 23, 2010 2:50 am I really doubt it. He said he's delivering four movies, so that's what we're getting.

[]:             Quote Post by Ramiel » Fri Jul 23, 2010 2:54 am We're not really following along the original timeline. The angels from the original series that made it into NME have been presented in the correct order, all of them (er, bar Sachiel and possibly Bardiel, depending on how you look at it) have been changed quite a bit and there were still other angels that were left out. Also, characters have been changed a lot. I don't see why it can't turn out to have a longer timeframe. But that's probably just the wide eyed optimist side of me speaking. Though I'd love three more movies, I think we're likely to just get two short ones.

[]:             Quote Post by KLVTZ » Fri Jul 23, 2010 2:56 am Ramiel wrote: We're not really following along the original timeline. The angels from the original series that made it into NME have been presented in the correct order, all of them (er, bar Sachiel and possibly Bardiel, depending on how you look at it) have been changed quite a bit and there were still other angels that were left out. Also, characters have been changed a lot. I don't see why it can't turn out to have a longer timeframe. But that's probably just the wide eyed optimist side of me speaking. Though I'd love three more movies, I think we're likely to just get two short ones.

[]:             Quote Post by skikes » Fri Jul 23, 2010 2:37 pm From a financial point of view I think they would be crazy not to continue it after the 4 movies. The 4 movies could be just one of many arcs. They have put the effort into making an "accessible" eva universe without all the complications of the series. A universe that fans wouldn't have a fear of continueing. NGE stands alone as one of the greatest anime series of all time. A continuation would cheapen it... possibly. But rebuild could just go on and on. Giving fans what they want, more from their favorite characters without damaging the integrity NGE.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Fri Jul 23, 2010 3:06 pm Hahahaha, yea right.

[]:             Quote Post by Thunder Heart Mk.3 » Fri Jul 23, 2010 4:33 pm Would it be a bad business decision? Probably not since it would just further milk that fat and happy money cow that is the Evangelion franchise. As for story line wise... I would hope that if there was indeed a fifth movie coming up, I personally would want it to answer the multitudes of unanswered questions that 3.0 and 4.0 are going to leave behind. Then again it really isn't Evangelion without the mystery and speculation.

[]:             Quote Post by KLVTZ » Fri Jul 23, 2010 5:21 pm Thunder Heart Mk.3 wrote: Would it be a bad business decision? Probably not since it would just further milk that fat and happy money cow that is the Evangelion franchise. As for story line wise... I would hope that if there was indeed a fifth movie coming up, I personally would want it to answer the multitudes of unanswered questions that 3.0 and 4.0 are going to leave behind. Then again it really isn't Evangelion without the mystery and speculation.

[]:             Quote Post by Velefor » Sat Jul 24, 2010 2:07 am There won't be a fifth movie. They might do remake of old NGE or screen some fishy alternative universe school comedy with GOS-like plot, but even this is highly unlikable. Probably all we would get after rebuild is few more games.There are two reason for that. First one, surprise-surprise, is financial. It's not that profitable to be screening one in two years movie sequence for limited fan audience. And even more importantly because the time is so extended financial risks became way to high. Now the second one. Anno as an author and director probably doesn't want to be stuck with one fictional universe all his life. Especially since Eva-verse isn't designed to be infinitively expandable like for say Star Wars, Battletech or Warhummer. Each Eva-story is destined to come into third impact culmination and logically end there.

[]:             Quote Post by Lonecow12 » Sat Jul 24, 2010 7:54 pm I've said it before but, if there is anything after Rebuild it will be Evangelion Anima TV series so they can keep the franchise alive like Gundam for years.

[]:             Quote Post by facespace » Sun Jul 25, 2010 12:58 pm I would hate to see Evangelion becoming something similar to Gundam. In my opinion Sunrise only puts out one decent Gundam series/movie every decade. With that said I would hate to have Evangelion over-saturated with a bunch of other Eva titles (with the majority of them not being memorable). I would like to see them find a creative way to keep the series alive, but not in the sense that Sunrise keeps Gundam alive. Also, Gundam seems like a ploy to sell toys and model kits.
